input: Schedule a dentist appointment on June 15th at 2pm

expected.title: Dentist appointment
assumptions: Year assumed to be current year

notes: Fully specified input. Both parsers should agree.

input: Book a team lunch on March 3rd at noon for 90 minutes

expected.title: Team lunch
assumptions: Year assumed to be current year

notes: All fields explicit. Strong baseline test.

input: Add a meeting on 2025-08-20 at 10:30am for 45 minutes
notes: ISO date format. Parser should handle both ISO and natural date formats.

input: Remind me about the board presentation on December 1st at 9am

expected.title: Board presentation
assumptions: Year assumed to be current year

notes: Tests title extraction with 'remind me about' prefix.

input: Set up a call on the 5th at 3pm

expected.title: Call
assumptions: Month assumed to be current month
Year assumed to be current year

notes: Partial date — day only. Rule-based likely misses month context; LLM should infer current month.

input: Schedule a grooming appointment for Sophie in 2 weeks
notes: Reference input from the app spec. Both parsers should handle 'in 2 weeks'.

input: Create a haircut appointment in 10 days at 11am
notes: Numeric day offset with explicit time.

input: Book a flight check-in reminder in 3 days
notes: Relative date, no time. Common reminder pattern.

input: Schedule a vet appointment for Luna a month from now
notes: 'A month from now' is ambiguous — could mean same day next calendar month or +30 days. Parsers likely differ.

input: Add a review meeting in a week at 2pm for an hour
notes: 'In a week' = +7 days. All fields inferable.

expected.title: Lunch
expected.dateInterpretation: The coming Friday (could be this Friday or next Friday depending on current day)
assumptions: 'Friday' assumed to mean the next upcoming Friday

notes: Classic ambiguity: if today is Thursday, 'friday' is tomorrow. If today is Saturday, 'friday' is 6 days away. No 'next' or 'this' qualifier.

expected.title: Lunch
expected.dateInterpretation: The Friday of the current week
assumptions: 'This Friday' interpreted as Friday within current week

notes: 'This' signals the current week's Friday. Still ambiguous if run on a Saturday — does 'this friday' mean yesterday or next week?

expected.title: Lunch
expected.dateInterpretation: The Friday of next week (7+ days away)
assumptions: 'Next Friday' interpreted as the Friday of next week, not the nearest Friday

notes: 'Next friday' vs 'this friday' — humans disagree on whether 'next friday' means the coming Friday or the one after. Strong parser differentiator.

expected.title: Gym
assumptions: 'Monday' assumed to mean next upcoming Monday

notes: Bare weekday with no qualifier. Rule-based and LLM will likely apply different default logic.

input: coffee with Alex sometime next week

expected.title: Coffee with Alex
assumptions: 'Sometime next week' is vague — defaulted to Wednesday
Time defaulted to morning coffee time

notes: 'Sometime' makes this inherently unresolvable to a single date. Any parser must pick an arbitrary day. Good test of how parsers handle deliberate vagueness.

expected.title: Standup
assumptions: Recurring 'every monday' treated as single next occurrence
Duration inferred from standup context as 30 min

notes: Recurring language but treated as single event. Tests whether parser flags recurrence vs silently drops it.
Date is clear, time is missing. Tests default time fallback.

expected.title: Call mom
assumptions: No date found — defaulted to tomorrow
Duration guessed as 30 min for a phone call

notes: Minimal input. No date or time. Tests pure defaults. LLM may infer 30 min for a call; rule-based will likely default to 60 min.

expected.title: Team offsite
assumptions: Duration guessed as full day (480 min) for an offsite

notes: 'Offsite' implies a full-day event. LLM should catch this; rule-based will default to 60 min. Good differentiator.

expected.title: Dentist
assumptions: 'Next month' resolved to first weekday of next calendar month

notes: 'Next month' is vague — no specific day. Parsers must pick a day within next month.

expected.title: Pick up prescription
assumptions: Duration guessed as 15 min for a pickup errand

notes: Short errand. LLM should infer short duration; rule-based defaults to 60 min.
Fully specified except time. Clean missing-time baseline.

expected.title: Yoga class
assumptions: Duration defaulted to 60 min — yoga typically 60-90 min

notes: Context could suggest morning time; LLM might pick 08:00 or 09:00.

expected.title: Dinner with parents
assumptions: Duration guessed as 90 min for a family dinner

notes: 'Dinner' implies evening. LLM should infer ~18:00; rule-based may default to 09:00 if 'dinner' keyword isn't handled.

expected.title: Haircut
assumptions: No date found — defaulted to tomorrow
Duration guessed as 45 min for a haircut

notes: 'After work' is context-dependent. Parsers must interpret this as late afternoon. Strong LLM vs rule-based differentiator.

expected.title: Morning run
assumptions: No date found — defaulted to tomorrow
Duration guessed as 45 min for a run

notes: 'Morning' is present but ambiguous — 06:00? 07:00? 09:00? Convention varies.

input: Quick catch-up with Sarah tomorrow at 3pm
notes: 'Quick' implies short. Rule-based will likely default to 60 min; LLM should infer 15–30 min.

input: Long strategy session with the leadership team on Friday morning
notes: 'Long' is highly subjective — 2h? 3h? 4h? Both parsers will guess differently.
No explicit duration. 'Dinner' implies ~90 min socially but rule-based won't know this.

expected.title: Meeting
assumptions: 'In 30 minutes' interpreted as start time offset from now, not duration

notes: Critical ambiguity: 'in 30 minutes' means START TIME, not duration. Both parsers may misread this as duration.

input: Book a physio appointment next Monday at 10am for an hour
notes: Fully specified appointment. Strong baseline.

expected.title: Eye exam
assumptions: Month assumed to be current month

notes: Partial date with explicit time.

assumptions: Recurring 'every other Thursday' treated as single next occurrence

notes: Biweekly recurrence. Parser should flag it cannot store recurrence and treat as single event.

input: Add team standup next Monday at 9am for 30 minutes
notes: Fully specified. Reference input from the app spec.

expected.title: 1:1 with manager
assumptions: '4' interpreted as 4pm (16:00) due to meeting context

notes: 'At 4' — AM or PM? Meeting context suggests PM. Tests AM/PM inference without explicit marker.

input: Product demo call with client on Thursday at 11
notes: 'At 11' — clearly AM. No ambiguity.

expected.title: Call the bank
assumptions: Duration guessed as 30 min for a phone call

notes: 'Remind me to' prefix should be stripped. Standard reminder pattern.

expected.title: Pick up kids from school
assumptions: Duration guessed as 30 min for pickup

notes: Non-round time (3:15). Tests minute parsing.

expected.title: Anniversary dinner
assumptions: No date found — defaulted to tomorrow
Duration guessed as 120 min for anniversary dinner

notes: Extremely minimal input. No date, time, or duration. Parser has almost nothing to work with.

input: Weekly team sync on Mondays at 10am

expected.title: Weekly team sync
assumptions: Recurring weekly pattern not stored — treated as single next occurrence

Duration guessed as 15 min for an admin task

notes: Recurrence + action phrase. Title extraction must strip 'remind me every friday at 4pm to'.
No title at all. Parser must handle empty title gracefully.

expected.title: Dinner
assumptions: No date found — defaulted to tomorrow

notes: Single-word input. Absolute minimum viable input. Strong edge case.

expected.title: Something
assumptions: 'Later' is too vague to resolve — defaulted to tomorrow

notes: Deliberately unresolvable temporal reference. Parser should note the vagueness explicitly in assumptions.

expected.title: Event
assumptions: 'ASAP' interpreted as today at next available hour

input: Lunch with the team on the last Friday of the month

expected.title: Lunch with the team
expected.dateInterpretation: The final Friday of the current month
assumptions: 'Last Friday of the month' requires calendar arithmetic

notes: 'Last Friday of the month' requires knowing the month layout. Rule-based will almost certainly fail this.

input: Meet with Jake the day after the offsite

expected.title: Meet with Jake
assumptions: 'Day after the offsite' cannot be resolved without context

notes: Reference to an external, unknown event. No parser can resolve this correctly. Should fail or flag dependency.

expected.title: Birthday party
assumptions: No date found — defaulted to tomorrow
Time guessed as evening (18:00) for a party

notes: Two-word event with no temporal info. Tests social context inference.

expected.title: Meeting
assumptions: Rescheduling intent detected — treated as new event at 4pm
Original 3pm event not modified (no edit capability)

notes: Rescheduling command, not a creation command. Parser may misinterpret intent entirely. Should ideally surface an error or flag.

input: Can you block some time for deep work next week?

expected.title: Deep work
assumptions: 'Some time' is vague — defaulted to 2 hours
'Next week' resolved to Wednesday as midpoint

notes: Conversational phrasing with 'can you'. LLM handles this better; rule-based may fail to extract any useful fields.

expected.title: Quarterly business review
assumptions: No date found — defaulted to tomorrow
Duration guessed as 4 hours for a quarterly review

notes: 'Quarterly' implies scale but not a specific date. LLM may try to guess end-of-quarter; rule-based defaults to tomorrow.

expected.title: Happy hour
assumptions: No date found — defaulted to today or tomorrow

notes: Cultural idiom with implied time. LLM may infer 17:00; rule-based has no signal.

expected.title: Fix the bug
assumptions: No date found — defaulted to tomorrow
Input is a task, not a calendar event

notes: This is a task/todo, not a calendar event. Should probably fail or be categorised differently. Tests parser intent detection.
Non-linguistic input. Parser must not crash. Should return a graceful fallback with all assumptions flagged.

input: Lunch with the London team at 12 on the 31st of February

expected.title: Lunch with London team
expected.dateInterpretation: Invalid date — February 31st does not exist
assumptions: Parser should flag or reject the date
Time and title parseable; date is the failure point

notes: Deliberately invalid date. Parser must handle calendar impossibilities without crashing. Good robustness test.
